C[opex: Hi! Have a good game
C[opex: Thus far all was OK, but this move feels low. I think that k4 keeps better balance of territory and influence

C[opex: I've heard some dans saying that when you see a double wing construction, you should seriously consider invading (c17). Indeed: invading at c14 provokes c15 answer and a running group. c17 gets you safe territory in sente, so that though white builds wall, you get the chance to reduce it.
C[opex: A natural response to the kick

opex: Here the placement of k16 is ideal: three-space extension from a wall of two. So the kick helps white. Without p17 there are options to enclose the corner with q18 threatening m17, or directly invade at m17. Now they are gone.
C[opex: Very shy. Yes, this move builds moyo, but it also lets white build his. More to it, white's is bigger. So go for reducing. Imagine playing tengen :)

C[opex: Interesting response. Standard are r16 to let white get a few points (r16 p18 q18 q17 r18 p16 s17 tenuki), p18 to let white live but get a peep into white's territory, or q17 to put the most pressure on white stone.
opex: Though i didnt find a way to prove that a standard response was better :))
C[opex: Why would you want to play ko here..

C[opex: Nice that you finally invaded, but usually invasions come much earlier, before yose is played. Consider this instead of m13, for example. There was much more space to run. Now even if white doesn't kill black, he may seal you in, so that you live small and don't eat enough territory to win.
C[opex: Thank you for the game
C[wiking: Hello, good luck and have a nice game

C[wiking: I realize this is an odd request, but do you have any tip to offer on how to make make the group F13 live?
C[cleatoma: I can't see a way you can do it.
cleatoma: Even if it were your move. You need F12 to separate E12 from your other possible eyes, and you need lots of stones to make G12/G13/F11 into a second eye.

C[quirck: Hi! Have a good game :)
wiking: Hello. I'm sure I will and a good game to you to.
quirck: If you want, put Malkovich comments as to what you think about my moves, what you want to achieve with your moves, etc. It'll be interesting to read after the game.
C[wiking: Hello. I'm sure I will and a good game to you to.
quirck: If you want, put Malkovich comments as to what you think about my moves, what you want to achieve with your moves, etc. It'll be interesting to read after the game.
C[wiking: I will, though my comments perhaps is of less value to you than yours are to me.
wiking: Some nice features on this go server.
C[quirck: They show how you think, and it's always interesting :)
quirck: Not much to comment yet, all moves are pretty standard. I got a base so that black can't build a moyo on the top. The k17 group can't be considered 100% safe, but for now it's ok.

quirck: Quite expectable. I think i won't go for something fancy like pincers. Two reasons here: my favorite sequence (see above) ends in gote, so I won't have time to place k4. Second, some pros teach that pincer is good when it has support from the neighbour corner. In this case, q16 is black, and the support is from d4. So answer is better. So o3
quirck: If black goes for standard joseki
quirck: he will be low, and r9-q16 pair won't work efficiently. But if black replies at q10, i may have to worry about right side moyo.
wiking: Why didn't you go for C3?
C[wiking: Bigger than F2 without loosing the corner
C[quirck: You continue to keep the initiative.. This way i'm gonna lose
C[quirck: Huh? Why not continue to build something like f15 or n10?.. Or protect r16.. This threatens to steal my base, yes, but I have space to run to...
C[wiking: Was hoping I'd be before you in that corner
quirck: i'd've chosen r15 for influence, given how r10 is placed..
C[quirck: Interestingly, was it misclick, or you changed your mind..
C[quirck: r13 is a bit inconsistent with r17. While r17 defends the corner and aims on territory, r13 attacks the group but leaves the corner in danger
quirck: Who sould have struggled for life in the corner?...
C[quirck: Hm.. This is unusual for tripod shape
quirck: s19 seems to kill here
quirck: I'm considering whether to let you live by playing s16 and live myself immediately in return
quirck: Let's try out s19, though :)
C[quirck: Hehe, so it's a trap? :)
quirck: But on a closer look, it seems I can afford s16 :)
C[wiking: Some times my move ends up in a total different area of the board than I intended when I play with my Iphone. Ever happened to you?
C[quirck: No, I don't have iphone:)
C[quirck: An easy territory for me..
C[quirck: So you threw n8 in and now let me cut it.. It means n8 was deep or you should agree on mutual destruction n8/m9
C[wiking: Darn, how can I defend myself against that?
C[quirck: Not necessary now, g18 area is bigger
C[quirck: Note that p19 now can connect q19 to safety
C[quirck: Don't follow me! Take sente when you feel that there's a bigger place. h13 doesn't give you any points at all
C[quirck: 0.5 points instead of sente q14, e1, q6
quirck: Interesting, that in the beginning you were more willing to control the flow of the game
C[quirck: This is a -1 point move. The group is dead already. And note the eye shape with white stone already in place
C[quirck: Though, e1 might not be sente :)
C[quirck: Such moves should be used as a ko threats
wiking: Unless you played that move
quirck: Then there is a choice: win ko or not let the group resurrect
C[quirck: Thank you for the game
quirck: Time to read comments :) If you have any questions, feel free to mail me:)
quirck: Oh, so there are questions in the malkovich... I'll try to answer them here
quirck: As for move 10: q10 is not for territory, it's for moyo
wiking: Thanks a lot. I need to sleep now. Will read tomorrow.
quirck: Move 14: I don't know any joseki with this move
quirck: And it was for this reason I replied to c4 with c5
quirck: As for c3, with hoshi d4 i from the beginning aim more to influence than for territory. I may willingly give you the corner building wall facing k4 in return
quirck: The correct way to stop white depends on the presence of black stone at c13
quirck: If there is no stone, like in our game, then
quirck: Sorry, I meant c12, not c13 :)
quirck: If c12 is black, then
quirck: white can't go further to a14 since black cuts at a12
quirck: Actually you might want to read http://senseis.xmp.net/?Monkey and http://senseis.xmp.net/?MonkeyJump%2FIntoHighPosition on this topic
quirck: Well, that's it for the time being. Hope it helps :)
wiking: Thanks for your comments and thoughts. There were two or three undos on my behalf all from slips of fingers. I never ask for undo when I realize I made a mistake. That's like cheating.
wiking: I don't know any josekis. You think I should study them?

quirck: Well, it may be fun to learn through playing, and this way you may even discover something more powerful than existing joseki variants. Or confuse your opponent with unexpected move, thus putting him into uncomfortable position where he is likely to make mistakes... Sounds like fun. As for me, I learnt about 5-6 joseki from the beginning, and then, every time i was confronted with an unknown response, i went and looked up the standard sequence. This way i picked up about 10 more josekis, but still i often forget some moves. So I won't give any recommendation :) Usually people say that you should _study_ joseki, not _learn_ them by heart. So maybe it'll be helpful to take joseki as a way to just look up how to play in certain situations, that is, to see what is considered best play nowadays.

quirck: I was a bit surprised with the upper right corner.. Do you know eye shapes? They're definitely worth learning as they have been thoroughly studied (unlike joseki) and save a great deal of reading:)
wiking: I guess I was desperately hoping you would make a mistake in that corner.
quirck: Ah :)) That happens, I've recently overlooked a fatal atari on my huge group :D I was too lazy to count dame, and I paid for it :D

C[opex: You've put too many low stones. Usually it's recommended to keep balance between territory and influence

C[opex: This move only solidifies me: k4 becomes ideal extension from the wall of two stones. Better to play elsewhere, leaving h3 as a possible invasion. If you want to protect the corner, d2 or even e2 may be played.
C[opex: This hazama tobi provokes to go through. I think f16 is ok here. One common variation: wf16 d16 d17 c15 b16 g14
C[opex: So you've put g15 and now push me on to cut it

C[opex: Again same shape (m16-o14), and yet again you let me cut that good stone. Don't! It's a wedge into black's moyo, if black cuts it, he'll be very happy. n16 instead of this move allows to stay connected (also note good shapes o14-n16-o17, o17-n16-l16).

C[opex: You're very kind. Just grab some territory, nothing more.. Jump towards the center, reduce black! or even go and invade his corner. The longer you don't do anything about the moyo, the more difficult it becomes to prevent all this moyo becoming solid territory.
C[opex: I don't like attaching here.. I'd go g8 or something
C[opex: I hope you won't make this move in the future :) e1 is correct
C[opex: Eh, empty triangle.. Not now. It's just 1 point reverse sente, while r18 still threatens 8 points sente monkey jump.
C[opex: Same: too cheap for now

C[opex: Correct way to react here is o18. Then black must play p19, then either q19 q18 n19 p18 n18, or n19 directly, and if black ataris n18, you cut him with counter-atari q19, and that small group will hardly find eyes.
o18 works well when p17 stone is in place. If it wasn't the case, the correct answer would be n18.
